nodes.label: AI as second opinion for critical decisions
nodes.grounding_span: AI was often used as a second opinion in this case, to either agree with or disagree with my findings

nodes.label: AI for experimental design simulation
nodes.label_negative: Inefficient trial and error without AI
nodes.grounding_span: AI tools helped me simulate different approaches to dosing and timing, which saved us from pursuing less efficient trial and error paths

nodes.label: AI for nuanced imaging analysis
nodes.grounding_span: AI assisted analysis proved to be a great addition to QC, where any diagnostic images that had unclear or ambiguous features were analyzed by AI as a secondary check to detect subtle changes

nodes.label: AI for behavioral vision testing
nodes.grounding_span: I would want AI to handle the behavioral side of vision testing, where we track eye movements in response to light therapy and moving patterns

nodes.label: AI reliability and error-proneness
nodes.grounding_span: It did not replace our expertise, and often made meaningful errors

nodes.label: Experimental accuracy and ethical integrity
nodes.grounding_span: it was incredibly important that the right conclusions were drawn from the imaging stage of the study

nodes.label: Research efficiency and resource optimization
nodes.grounding_span: saved us from pursuing less efficient trial and error paths

nodes.label: Precision and consistency in measurement
nodes.grounding_span: capture micro-movements with high precision and quantify them consistently

nodes.label: Trust in AI as complementary tool with verification
nodes.grounding_span: AI was often used as a second opinion in this case, to either agree with or disagree with my findings

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI due to errors
nodes.grounding_span: It did not replace our expertise, and often made meaningful errors

nodes.label: Aspiration for AI in behavioral testing
nodes.grounding_span: I would want AI to handle the behavioral side of vision testing

nodes.label: Systematic validation and verification approach
nodes.grounding_span: I often would treat the AI as a blinded second reader and verify its calls against masked human grading and predefined QC thresholds

nodes.label: Domain-specific selective adoption of AI
nodes.grounding_span: I never leaned on AI as much for this aspect of our study. The main reason is that these tests heavily rely on direct observation of eye movement
